---
title: eeg-web-ble
description: Web Bluetooth transport for EEG headbands in the browser (built-in Muse; open to more devices).
---

## Bluetooth vs built-in devices

- **Transport:** **`BleTransport`** (`src/transport/`) — Web Bluetooth session and
  **`HeadbandFrameV1`** assembly; uses **`HeadbandTransport`** types from `eeg-web`.
- **Built-in protocol:** **`MuseBleDevice`** (`src/devices/muse/`) — Muse 2 / Muse S
  classic + Athena GATT handling. Other headsets: new `src/devices/...` modules or
  `new BleTransport({ device })`.

## What This Package Is

This package owns:

- Web Bluetooth–oriented transport and frame emission (`BleTransport`)
- a **built-in** Muse implementation (classic BLE and Athena protocol)
- optional **other headsets** via `device` on `BleTransport`

It intentionally depends on `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` for shared transport
and frame contracts. **Import from the package root** — do not rely on deep paths.

## When To Use It

Use this package when your app needs:

- browser-side discovery and streaming for **supported** headsets (Muse 2, Muse S classic, Muse S Athena out of the box)
- live EEG streaming over Web Bluetooth
- normalized headband frames for app logic or downstream analysis

Install it alongside `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web`.

Contributors and vendors can extend transport for additional hardware; see
[Contributing EEG Transports](/maintainers/contributing-eeg-transports).

## Install

<CodeGroup>

```bash pnpm
pnpm add @elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble @elata-biosciences/eeg-web
```

```bash npm
npm install @elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble @elata-biosciences/eeg-web
```

</CodeGroup>

## Requirements

- Node.js `>= 20` for build and tests
- a browser with Web Bluetooth support, typically Chrome or Edge
- a secure context such as `https://` or `localhost`

## Usage

```ts
import { BleTransport } from "@elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble";
import { AthenaWasmDecoder, initEegWasm } from "@elata-biosciences/eeg-web";

// Initialize WASM before connecting (required for AthenaWasmDecoder).
await initEegWasm();

const transport = new BleTransport({
  deviceOptions: {
    athenaDecoderFactory: () => new AthenaWasmDecoder()
  }
});

transport.onFrame = (frame) => {
  console.log(frame.eeg.samples.length);
};

transport.onStatus = (status) => {
  console.log(status.state, status.reason);
};

// startStreaming() calls connect() + start() in one step.
await transport.startStreaming();
```

If you need finer-grained control (e.g. reconnect without re-pairing), you
can call `connect()` and `start()` separately — but both are required;
omitting `start()` produces silence with no error.

## Key Exports

- `BleTransport`
- `BleDeviceLike` (adapter contract for custom headset integrations)
- `MuseBleDevice` (default built-in device)
- transport and frame types

## Platform Caveats

- Safari and iOS do not provide usable Web Bluetooth support for this workflow.
- Athena startup requires an `athenaDecoderFactory`.
- Muse firmware variants may differ in command behavior.

For Safari and iOS, prefer a native BLE shell, companion bridge, or hybrid
WebView strategy with `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` frame contracts as the
boundary.
